title: Michael Cliffe

infobox.value: Myer Cliffe (1903-03-06)6 March 1903  [Sheffield](/wiki/Sheffield "Sheffield"), [West Riding of Yorkshire](/wiki/West_Riding_of_Yorkshire "West Riding of Yorkshire"), England
9 August 1964(1964-08-09) (aged 61)  [Smithfield](/wiki/Smithfield,_London "Smithfield, London"), [City of London](/wiki/City_of_London "City of London"), England

**[Member of Parliament](/wiki/Member_of_Parliament_(United_Kingdom) "Member of Parliament (United Kingdom)") for [Shoreditch and Finsbury](/wiki/Shoreditch_and_Finsbury "Shoreditch and Finsbury")**  [1958](/wiki/1958_Shoreditch_and_Finsbury_by-election "1958 Shoreditch and Finsbury by-election")–1964 | Succeeded by [Ronald Brown](/wiki/Ronald_Brown_(English_politician) "Ronald Brown (English politician)")

(6 March 1903 – 9 August 1964) was a British clothing industry worker and politician, who was a [Member of Parliament](/wiki/Member_of_Parliament_(United_Kingdom) "Member of Parliament (United Kingdom)") in inner London for six years.
["No. 39751"](https://www.thegazette.co.uk/London/issue/39751/page/311). *[The London Gazette](/wiki/The_London_Gazette "The London Gazette")*. 13 January 1953. p. 311.

**Michael Cliffe** (6 March 1903 – 9 August 1964) was a British clothing industry worker and politician, who was a [Member of Parliament](/wiki/Member_of_Parliament_(United_Kingdom) "Member of Parliament (United Kingdom)") in inner London for six years.

## Working life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Cliffe&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Working life")]

Cliffe, was born in [Sheffield](/wiki/Sheffield "Sheffield") and went to Holliscroft School, leaving to go into the clothing industry as a tailor's presser. He was active in the [National Union of Tailors and Garment Workers](/wiki/National_Union_of_Tailors_and_Garment_Workers "National Union of Tailors and Garment Workers") and was elected to the Union's National Executive. He moved to London to live in [Finsbury](/wiki/Finsbury "Finsbury") where he became active in local politics as a member of the [Labour Party](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)"); in 1949 he was elected to [Finsbury Borough Council](/wiki/Finsbury_Borough_Council "Finsbury Borough Council") from West Finsbury ward, at the top of the poll in a six-member ward. On the last day of 1952 he formally adopted the first name 'Michael' by [deed poll](/wiki/Deed_poll "Deed poll").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Municipal experience

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Cliffe&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Municipal experience")]

After local ward boundary changes, Cliffe was elected in Central ward from 1953, but then was elected an [Alderman](/wiki/Alderman "Alderman") (his wife Sophia, née Whitesman, followed him onto the council); he remained an Alderman until his death. He was chairman of the Housing Committee, and when the building work was completed he was allocated a flat in the [Golden Lane Estate](/wiki/Golden_Lane_Estate "Golden Lane Estate"). He was chosen as Mayor of Finsbury for the year 1956-1957 and was then Chairman of the Policy and Resources Committee (the unofficial Leader of the Council).

## Byelection

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Cliffe&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Byelection")]

As Chairman of Shoreditch and Finsbury [Constituency Labour Party](/wiki/Constituency_Labour_Party "Constituency Labour Party") when the local Member of Parliament [Victor Collins](/wiki/Victor_Collins,_Baron_Stonham "Victor Collins, Baron Stonham") took a [life peerage](/wiki/Life_peerage "Life peerage") in the first appointments in 1958, Cliffe was selected as his successor as Labour candidate for the constituency of [Shoreditch and Finsbury](/wiki/Shoreditch_and_Finsbury "Shoreditch and Finsbury"). Despite a rumbustious campaign in which the police had to be called to one of his meetings and arrested four men, Cliffe comfortably retained the seat on a low turnout in the byelection.

## Parliamentary life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Cliffe&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Parliamentary life")]

A quiet and rare speaker in the [House of Commons](/wiki/British_House_of_Commons "British House of Commons"), Cliffe was active in supporting his constituents. He joined a delegation to the Home Secretary from the Street Bookmakers' Federation in 1960, saying he wanted to be better informed of their views in advance of the debate on a Gambling Bill. He was Chairman of the [Warsaw Ghetto Uprising](/wiki/Warsaw_Ghetto_Uprising "Warsaw Ghetto Uprising") Memorial Committee and in 1961 called attention to increasing votes for fascist candidates, declaring "anything that spells Fascism must be rooted out and destroyed".

When tension grew over the [Berlin Wall](/wiki/Berlin_Wall "Berlin Wall") in 1961, Cliffe was one of four Labour MPs who demanded the [recall of Parliament](/wiki/Recall_of_Parliament "Recall of Parliament"), and later wrote a letter to [President Kennedy](/wiki/President_Kennedy "President Kennedy") and [Nikita Khrushchev](/wiki/Nikita_Khrushchev "Nikita Khrushchev") urging hasty negotiations and a moratorium on nuclear testing. He had been intending to continue his Parliamentary career, but was taken ill and died in [St Bartholomew's Hospital](/wiki/St_Bartholomew%27s_Hospital "St Bartholomew's Hospital") in August 1964, aged 61. Finsbury Metropolitan Borough Council named a 25-storey block of flats after him on the [Finsbury Estate](/wiki/Finsbury_Estate "Finsbury Estate") simply named Michael Cliffe House.
